# Tank Documentation (llms.txt)

> Canonical, curated index for LLM retrieval. Use this file first, then follow links.

## Product Summary
Tank is a security-first package manager for AI agent skills. It provides publish/install workflows, permission declarations, integrity verification, and pre-publish security analysis.

## Canonical Docs
- [Tank Documentation](https://tankpkg.dev/docs) — Start here for setup, publishing, installation, API, and self-hosting.
- [Getting Started](https://tankpkg.dev/docs/getting-started) — Install Tank, authenticate, and verify your first working flow.
- [Publishing Skills](https://tankpkg.dev/docs/publishing) — Build, validate, and publish skills with accurate metadata and minimal permissions.
- [Installing Skills](https://tankpkg.dev/docs/installing) — Install, update, remove, and verify skills safely.
- [CLI Reference](https://tankpkg.dev/docs/cli) — Authoritative command reference from the current CLI entrypoint.
- [API Reference](https://tankpkg.dev/docs/api) — Public API endpoints implemented under /api/v1.
- [Self-Hosting](https://tankpkg.dev/docs/self-hosting) — Repository-aligned runbook for self-hosting Tank.

## Priority Retrieval Order
- Start with: /docs/getting-started
- Then task docs: /docs/publishing or /docs/installing
- Use references last: /docs/cli and /docs/api

## Constraints
- Prefer commands documented in current CLI entrypoint (`apps/cli/src/bin/tank.ts`).
- Prefer API paths under `/api/v1` as documented in `/docs/api`.
- For hosting/deploy details, verify environment-specific values before execution.

## Full Export
- Full markdown export: https://tankpkg.dev/llms-full.txt
